description: initial baseline at seq=2048; NaN loss at seq=2048 flagged; seq=1024 subset clean
20-step loss-descent confirmation at seq=1024; loss 3.93 → 1.97; canonical pre-experiment reference
async-collective XLA flag bundle; -25% regression; compute-fusion memory traffic blew up
attempted to pin out_shardings to fix step-1 recompile; tied-weight sharding collapsed to single-device; jit rejected mismatch; reverted
full activation remat; -22% TPS but -29% peak HBM (95%→67%); memory-first prep for batch growth; loss unchanged
doubled batch under full remat; +16.5% over exp3 but net -8.9% vs baseline; HBM 92% leaves no headroom for batch=4; chain refuted
selective remat; only +8.7% step time (vs full remat's +27.5%); -38% peak HBM (95%→62%); no forward doubling; 11.9 GiB free for batch growth
selective remat + batch=2; first experiment to beat baseline (+1.2% TPS); HBM 83% still has headroom
selective remat + batch=3; HBM 97.6% nearly OOM; per-token cost 33.7 µs (worse than batch=2's 32.3); memory pressure degrades per-token efficiency; batch=2 is the sweet spot

FIRST PALLAS EXPERIMENT. Swapped XLA SDPA for splash_attention via shard_map+call_jax; +2.7% over baseline (new best); loss match; HBM unchanged at seq=1024 (N² too small to matter); splash adds 122 ms custom-call op replacing part of convolution+loop fusion

splash + seq=2048 batch=1; *FIXED NaN* that was open since baseline (splash's online-max softmax is numerically stable); loss 3.26→1.50 clean descent; +1.9% TPS over baseline seq=1024; unblocks long-seq training
CompileTimeHbmOom at seq=2048 batch=2 even with bf16 CE saving ~2 GiB fp32 logits; OOM by 1.25 GiB (32.49G > 31.25G); need offload remat

offload_dot_with_no_batch_dims (host-offload) didn't reduce compile-time peak; OOM 32.48G of 31.25 (1.24G over) — same as checkpoint variant; XLA planner doesn't account for host-offload as freed HBM at compile time

NEW BEST +5.8% over baseline; splash + bf16 CE stack cleanly; loss trajectory identical to exp 8 (fp32 CE) despite bf16 log-softmax — Gemma 4's final_logit_softcapping=30.0 keeps logits bounded so bf16 CE is stable

async-collective XLA flag bundle revisited on best config (splash+bf16CE); still regresses (+12.7% step time); same mechanism as exp 1 — scheduler reorder breaks compute-fusion locality; permanently park these flags at this scale

splash+bf16 CE at seq=2048 b=1 (comparison to exp 9); TPS 31,960 — just below exp 12's 32,340 with same tokens/step; batch=2/seq=1024 edges batch=1/seq=2048 by ~1.2%; bf16 CE saved ~2.5% step time vs exp 9's fp32 CE
NEW BEST +7.0% over baseline; batch=3 fits now because splash+bf16 CE freed enough memory (vs exp 7 which OOM'd at 97.6%); HBM 98.78% right at ceiling; loss descent healthy; per-token 30.56 µs

set use_fused_bwd_kernel=True but left dq block-size params set; splash errored with 'Block sizes for dq kernel are not needed with a fused kernel'; XLA fallback fired for every layer — this number is XLA attention, not splash-fused-bwd; need to omit block_q_dq/block_kv_dq when fused_bwd=True (exp 17 branch does this); interesting side-finding: XLA fallback at this config is within 2 ms of splash

splash fused_bwd_kernel with correct block-size config; +0.9% over exp 12 / +6.8% over baseline; loss match; saved ~2.5 ms/step on bwd pass; on its own branch perfautoresearch/v6e4-20260423-exp17-splash-fused-bwd, merged to trunk
fused_bwd + batch=3 on top of exp 17; +8.0% over baseline; merged to trunk; per-exp md writeup TBD (gap in wiki filing)

exp25 stack at seq=2048 b=1; +0.9% over exp 14 (31,960); NOT new overall best (exp25 seq=1024 b=3 still leads at 33,372); per-step tight (σ<0.4ms); loss 3.27→1.98 clean; branch only (not merged — strictly dominated by exp25); writeup 2026-04-23-exp28-seq2048-exp25config-accepted.md

asymmetric splash blocks (block_q=1024, block_kv=512) vs exp25 symmetric 1024; -0.37% TPS (within noise, directionally worse); splash softmax-accumulator is sequential across KV tiles so smaller kv adds reload cost with no concurrency gain; symmetric 1024 is the sweet spot at seq=1024; not merged; writeup 2026-04-23-exp29-splash-asymmetric-rejected.md

latency_hiding_scheduler flag in isolation; -0.003% (statistically identical to exp25); clears this flag as a culprit for exp1/13/21 bundle regression — the bad actors are async_collective_fusion*; +15% compile cost (no production value); not merged; writeup 2026-04-23-exp30-latency-hiding-solo-potential.md

overlap_compute_collective_tc flag in isolation; -0.13% (flat/noise); combined with exp30 confirms async_collective_fusion* is the culprit in exp1/13/21 bundle; not merged; writeup 2026-04-23-exp31-overlap-compute-collective-tc-potential.md

2D mesh dp=2 tp=2 hybrid sharding; batch=3 compile-OOM by 1.15GiB (~same pattern as exp10/11); batch=2 ran at 12,711 TPS vs baseline's 30,570 at same global batch=4 — 2.4x slower; TP collectives dominate at 2 chips/axis; refuted, not merged; sharding.py got rank-guards + hybrid 2D logic; pallas_attention.py got 2D shard_map path; writeup 2026-04-23-exp32-2d-mesh-tp2-rejected.md

hand-written Pallas RMSNorm forward + hand-rolled custom_vjp backward for Gemma4RMSNorm (250+ calls/step); kernel numerics verified vs JAX reference to bf16 eps; loss trajectory preserved (step 4 loss 2.19 matches exp25); TPS 30,657 = -8.1% vs exp25; refuted — custom-call boundaries break XLA loop_fusion across norm/matmul seam so each norm gets 2x extra HBM round-trips; shard_map launch overhead adds ~25 ms/step for 250 calls; kernel correct but integration-cost story wrong; not merged; writeup 2026-04-23-exp33-pallas-rmsnorm-rejected.md
